(In 'e()', 'e' is an instance of Error) (fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6, line 1) [Log] [103:103] TODO: Advisory locking is only stubbed (fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6, line 1, x9) [Log] [221:221] TODO: Advisory locking is only stubbed (fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6, line 1, x9) [Log] Unexpected exit – "CheerpException@blob:https://link-foundation.github.io/fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6:1:12352…" (fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6, line 1) "CheerpException@blob:https://link-foundation.github.io/fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6:1:123524 ari@blob:https://link-foundation.github.io/fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6:1:65147 .wasm-function[2523]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[2524]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[2525]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[122]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[402]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[381]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[3345]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[3817]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[3774]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[3773]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[0]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[2378]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[2343]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[25]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[2]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[4]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[2360]@[wasm code] .wasm-function[2363]@[wasm code] aTr@blob:https://link-foundation.github.io/fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6:1:57641 aTt@blob:https://link-foundation.github.io/fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6:1:57508 .wasm-function[2518]@[wasm code] VF@blob:https://link-foundation.github.io/fcbce274-c412-442f-8739-43baa67740d6:1:59853" [Error] Source Map loading errors (x2) [Error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () (workbench.js.map, line 0) [Error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () (layout.contribution.darwin.js.map, line 0) ``` We already iterating with that specific problem for multiple pull requests now, may be we need some additional e2e tests, that will actually confirm everything is working. And we use real cargo run, not some fake commands. So we can use actual cargo run on first build, and on rebuild when *.rs files changes. Our code should contain as small number of custom solutions as possible, we should just synchronize transparently the WebVM and VSCode interface (file explorer, file editor, terminal). May be we should simplify and add as much debug logging as possible, so we 100% sure, what exactly goes wrong, and why it is not working, so we can fix it. We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions). If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration. If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code. Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
title: Repeated `cargo run` after changes in the `main.rs` file does not work
